questions.text: The property of metals by which they can be beaten into thin sheets is called:
questions.explanation: Gold and silver are the most malleable metals.

questions.text: The ability of metals to be drawn into thin wires is called:
questions.explanation: Gold is the most ductile metal; 1g of gold can be drawn into a 2km wire.

questions.text: Which of the following metals is a liquid at room temperature?
questions.explanation: Mercury is the only metal that is liquid at room temperature.

questions.text: Which non-metal is a good conductor of electricity?
questions.explanation: Graphite, an allotrope of carbon, has free electrons that allow it to conduct electricity.

questions.text: Metals that can be cut easily with a knife are:
questions.explanation: Alkali metals like Sodium and Potassium are soft and have low density.

questions.text: Oxides of metals are generally:
questions.explanation: Most metal oxides react with water to form alkalis.

questions.text: Metal oxides which react with both acids as well as bases to produce salt and water are called:
questions.explanation: Examples include Aluminum oxide (Al2O3) and Zinc oxide (ZnO).

questions.text: Which of the following metals does NOT react with cold water but reacts with hot water?
questions.explanation: Magnesium reacts with hot water to form magnesium hydroxide and hydrogen.

questions.text: Aqua regia is a freshly prepared mixture of concentrated HCl and concentrated HNO3 in the ratio:
questions.explanation: 3 parts HCl and 1 part HNO3 creates a highly corrosive liquid that can dissolve gold.

questions.text: The reactive series is a list of metals arranged in order of their:
questions.explanation: Potassium is at the top (most reactive) and Gold is at the bottom (least reactive).

questions.text: The bond formed by the transfer of electrons from a metal to a non-metal is called:
questions.explanation: Ionic compounds (electrovalent compounds) consist of oppositely charged ions.

questions.text: Ionic compounds have high melting points because:
options: They have weak forces of attraction
A large amount of energy is required to break the strong inter-ionic attraction
questions.explanation: Strong electrostatic forces hold the ions together in a crystal lattice.

questions.text: The science of extraction of metals from their ores and their purification is called:
questions.explanation: Metallurgy involves various physical and chemical processes.

questions.text: Cinnabar is an ore of:
questions.explanation: Cinnabar (HgS) is the main ore used to extract mercury.

questions.text: The process of heating sulphide ores in the presence of excess air is called:
questions.explanation: Roasting converts sulphide ores into metal oxides.

questions.text: Calcination is the process of heating ________ ores in limited supply of air.
questions.explanation: Carbonate ores are converted into oxides through calcination.

questions.text: The reaction of Iron(III) oxide with Aluminum used to join railway tracks is called:
questions.explanation: It is a highly exothermic reaction that produces molten iron.

questions.text: In electrolytic refining, the pure metal is deposited at the:
questions.explanation: The cathode is the negative electrode where metal ions gain electrons and deposit.

questions.text: An alloy is a:
options: Homogeneous mixture of two or more metals/non-metals
questions.explanation: Alloys are prepared to enhance the properties of pure metals.

questions.text: Brass is an alloy of:
questions.explanation: Brass is used for decorative items and musical instruments.

questions.text: Bronze is an alloy of:
questions.explanation: Bronze is known for its hardness and resistance to corrosion.

questions.text: The alloy used for soldering electrical wires is:
questions.explanation: Solder has a low melting point, making it ideal for joining wires.

questions.text: An amalgam is an alloy where one of the components is:
questions.explanation: Alloys of mercury are specifically called amalgams.

questions.text: Which of the following is a non-metal but is lustrous?
questions.explanation: Iodine is an exception among non-metals as it has a shiny appearance.

questions.text: Sodium is kept immersed in kerosene because:
options: It reacts vigorously with air and water
questions.explanation: Sodium is highly reactive and catches fire when exposed to moisture or oxygen.

questions.text: Which metal does NOT corrode easily in air?
questions.explanation: Gold is a noble metal and does not react with atmospheric gases.

questions.text: Galvanisation is a method of protecting iron from rusting by coating it with a thin layer of:
questions.explanation: Zinc acts as a sacrificial layer to prevent iron oxidation.

questions.text: The purest form of Gold is:
questions.explanation: 24 carat gold is very soft and is usually alloyed with copper or silver for jewelry.

questions.text: Food cans are coated with tin and not zinc because:
options: Zinc is more reactive than tin
Zinc is less reactive than tin
questions.explanation: Zinc would react with the organic acids in food, while tin is safer.

questions.text: Which of the following can be used to prevent corrosion?
questions.explanation: All these methods prevent the metal surface from coming in contact with moisture and air.

questions.text: Non-metals are generally ________ at room temperature.
questions.explanation: Most non-metals are gases or brittle solids (Bromine is the only liquid exception).

questions.text: Oxides of non-metals are usually:
questions.explanation: They react with water to form acids (e.g., SO2 forms H2SO3).

questions.text: Which metal is the best conductor of heat?
questions.explanation: Silver followed by Copper are the best thermal conductors.

questions.text: The sound produced when a metal strikes a hard surface is called:
questions.explanation: This property is why metals are used to make bells.

questions.text: Aluminum is highly reactive, yet it is used for cooking utensils because:
options: It forms a protective layer of aluminum oxide
It is a good conductor of heat
questions.explanation: The oxide layer prevents further corrosion, and its high conductivity makes it efficient for cooking.

questions.text: Which of the following does NOT happen when metals react with acids?
questions.explanation: Acid-metal reactions produce hydrogen, not oxygen.

questions.text: Magnesium ribbon burns in air with a dazzling ________ flame.
questions.explanation: The reaction produces MgO and a bright white light.

questions.text: The property of PVC that makes it useful for coating electrical wires is:
questions.explanation: PVC prevents electrical shocks as it does not conduct electricity.

questions.text: Rusting of iron requires both:
questions.explanation: The presence of both air (oxygen) and moisture is essential for rust formation.

questions.text: Stainless steel is an alloy of Iron with:
questions.explanation: Nickel and Chromium provide the high resistance to rusting.
